theory.intro: A parallelogram can be cut and rearranged into a rectangle with the same base and height, so its area is base times perpendicular height.
facts: **Area $=$ base $\times$ height**, where height is the **perpendicular** distance between the base and the opposite side.
The height is **not** the slanted side; it is measured at a right angle to the base.
Area is measured in square units: cm$^{2}$, m$^{2}$.
steps: Identify the base and the perpendicular height (the dashed line at a right angle).
Write the answer with squared units.
